description_long: This object is a wooden cabinet or shelving unit with a central vertical panel and two symmetrical side compartments, each featuring multiple horizontal and vertical dividers. The main structure appears to be constructed from medium-brown wood, with lighter wood or laminate accents and some white or off-white internal panels. The base is a solid, light-colored rectangular platform, likely simulating a tiled or stone surface. The compartments on either side of the central panel are open, suggesting they are designed for storage or display, and the lack of visible doors or handles indicates an open-shelf design. The overall construction is robust, with thick panels and a geometric, modular appearance. This piece would be suitable for use in public urban spaces such as libraries, lobbies, or waiting areas, where both durability and accessibility are important.
description: A robust, open-shelf wooden cabinet with a central panel and symmetrical side compartments, featuring both brown and white panels. The design is modular and suitable for public or urban environments.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central vertical panel and both open side compartments with visible dividers.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the central panel and the layered structure of the side compartments.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the back of the central panel and the open compartments.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the depth and the arrangement of the side compartments.
